body_txt: When Alice loves Bob and no one — not even Bob — is able to persuade her that their love isn't mutual. This is not just All Love Is Unrequited or Oblivious to Love . Bob is well aware of this one-sided crush, but his protests fall on deaf ears. He can tell Alice he isn't interested, sometimes violently , but she invariably fails to catch on . Her behavior often straddles the line between Clingy Jealous Girl , Stalker with a Crush or even Psycho Supporter , though she generally means no harm to Bob and also respects his ideals. But if she does start becoming an actual threat to Bob or other people close to him though, beware... If Alice has terrible taste in suitors (and she usually does), then her "hubby" or "wifey" will abuse that unconditional love to further their latest crazy scheme and never hesitate to abuse her when it fails , or if she gets too whiny. Y'know, because whining can cause headaches and stuff. And of course, the innocent (or more like oblivious) Alice will always come trotting back for more . If she has two neurons bobbing around in her skull, she'll be puzzled by hubby's "funny way" of showing love. If she has more than three, she'll eventually realize that Mr. Troubled, but Cute is just plain Trouble and move on , but she'll find it hard to resist falling back into his orbit. Expect Alice to be a headache for anyone in the center of a Love Triangle or Love Dodecahedron involving Bob. Third parties trying to convince her that she should move on will face replies like " You're Just Jealous " and other psychological barriers to reality and sanity . Either gender can be a Mad Lover, for a beloved of either gender. It's just more common to have females in the role because this is often a comedy trope, and a clueless woman clinging to an exasperated man is much funnier than the reverse. Some writers adore this dynamic and like to keep the 'relationship' alive by giving Alice an occasional moment of affection from Bob , just to make everyone wonder what's really going on. Compare Crazy in the Head, Crazy in the Bed , for instability depicted as an enticing kink. See also Hopeless Suitor and Rejection Affection , for less crazy examples of unrequited love from which this trope borrows some elements. Compare with the more serious Love Martyr , a character in an unequal relationship whose love is meant to be selfless and noble (or at least sympathetic) instead of crazy, funny, and/or stupid. May overlap with Abhorrent Admirer or its Sub-Trope , Attempted Homewrecker , if the target of their obsession is already in a relationship. Also compare Minion Shipping , Unholy Matrimony , and Fetishized Abuser . Contrast Vitriolic Best Buds , a friendship where one or both partners attack each other. Not to be confused with the short-lived TV series starring Jason Biggs and Judy Greer ; also not to be confused with the 1935 film Mad Love , (which doesn't use this trope) or the Batman: The Animated Series episode. Also not to be confused with the Pokémon ability Attract, whose Japanese name translates to "mad love".
